title: HoardCTI Threat Actor Schema
description: Standardized transport format for transferring threat actors, threat actor groups, their relationships (group membership, linked malware, IOCs, exploited CVEs, targets) and related news articles from the threat_actors source aggregator to the HoardCTI central server.

schema_version.description: Version of the HoardCTI threat actor schema this payload conforms to.

generated_at.description: UTC timestamp at which the aggregator produced this payload.

batch_id.description: Unique identifier for this transfer batch, used for idempotency and de-duplication on the server side.

count.description: Number of actor records in this payload. Must equal the length of the actors array if present.

actors.description: The threat actor and threat actor group records being transferred.

relationships.description: Relationships between actors in this payload and other entities: group membership, linked malware, IOCs, exploited CVEs, targeted sectors and countries.

articles.description: News articles covering the actors and groups in this payload.

source.title: Source
source.description: Identifies the aggregator repo/script that produced this payload.

name.description: Canonical name of the ingest source (e.g. 'mitre-attack', 'malpedia', 'etda').

repository.description: URL of the ingest repo that generated this payload.

feed_url.description: Upstream feed or API endpoint the data was aggregated from.

aggregator_version.description: Version of the aggregator script that produced this payload.

actor.title: Threat Actor Record
actor.description: A single threat actor: either an individual or a group (intrusion set). Individuals are tied to groups through member_of relationships rather than nesting.

id.description: Stable identifier for this actor within the feed. Relationships reference actors by this value, so it must not change between payloads.

type.description: Whether this record describes an individual person or a group (intrusion set).

name.description: Primary name the actor is tracked under (e.g. 'APT28', 'Lazarus Group').

aliases.description: Other names the actor is known by across vendors (e.g. 'Fancy Bear', 'STRONTIUM', 'Sofacy').

description.description: Free-text profile of the actor: history, notable operations, tradecraft.

first_seen.description: UTC timestamp of the earliest known activity attributed to this actor.

last_seen.description: UTC timestamp of the most recent known activity attributed to this actor.

activity_status.description: Whether the actor is believed to still be operating.

sophistication.description: Skill level of the actor, from script kiddie to nation-state innovator.

resource_level.description: Organizational level of the resources available to the actor.

roles.description: Roles the actor plays in operations.

goals.description: Stated or assessed objectives of the actor (e.g. 'theft of defense IP').

target_sectors.description: Industry sectors the actor is known to target (e.g. 'energy', 'finance', 'government').

target_countries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes of countries the actor is known to target.

techniques.description: MITRE ATT&CK techniques the actor is known to use. Prefer relationships of type 'uses' when confidence or sighting dates matter; this inline list is for simple technique inventories.

external_ids.description: Identifiers for this actor in external knowledge bases (e.g. MITRE ATT&CK group G0007, Malpedia, vendor tracking names).

references.description: External references: vendor profiles, reports, indictments, news coverage.

revoked.description: True if the upstream source has withdrawn this actor record (e.g. merged into another actor).

tags.description: Free-form tags applied by the aggregator (e.g. 'ransomware', 'apt').

attribution.title: Suspected Origin
attribution.description: Assessed origin of an actor. Attribution is rarely certain, so every claim carries a confidence rating.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code of the suspected country of origin.

statement.description: Free-text attribution statement (e.g. 'assessed to be GRU Unit 26165').

technique_id.description: MITRE ATT&CK technique or sub-technique identifier (e.g. 'T1566', 'T1566.001').

tactic.description: ATT&CK tactic the usage falls under (e.g. 'initial-access').

source_name.description: Knowledge base or vendor the identifier belongs to (e.g. 'mitre-attack', 'malpedia', 'crowdstrike').

id.description: The identifier in that knowledge base (e.g. 'G0007').

url.description: Direct URL to the actor's entry in that knowledge base.

relationship.title: Relationship
relationship.description: A directed, typed link from one entity to another. Membership: individual member_of group. Group-to-group: affiliated_with, cooperates_with, successor_of. Capability: uses / develops malware, tools, techniques or infrastructure; exploits CVEs; linked_to IOCs. Victimology: targets sectors, countries or organizations. Attribution: attributed_to a country or another actor.

id.description: Optional stable identifier for this relationship. When present it must be stable across batches (e.g. a UUIDv5 derived from type, source_ref and target_ref); when absent the server deduplicates by the (type, source_ref, target_ref) triple.

type.description: Semantics of the link, read as: source_ref <type> target_ref.

first_seen.description: UTC timestamp of the earliest observation supporting this relationship.

last_seen.description: UTC timestamp of the most recent observation supporting this relationship.

asserted_by.description: Who made this claim (e.g. 'mitre-attack', 'mandiant'), when it differs from the payload source.

entity_ref.title: Entity Reference
entity_ref.description: A reference to an entity on either end of a relationship. Actors in this payload are referenced by their actor id; external entities carry their natural identifier (CVE id, ATT&CK id, IOC value, ISO country code, or a name).

kind.description: What class of entity is being referenced.

id.description: Identifier of the entity: an actor id from this payload, a CVE id, an ATT&CK technique id, the IOC value itself, an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, or the canonical name for kinds without formal identifiers.

subtype.description: Finer classification within the kind: for ioc the indicator type (e.g. 'sha256', 'domain', 'ipv4', 'url'); for malware the class (e.g. 'ransomware', 'backdoor', 'loader').

name.description: Display name for the entity when the id alone is not human-readable.

article.title: News Article
article.description: A news article, blog post or report covering one or more actors in this payload. Articles are deduplicated server-side by url, so the same article seen in multiple batches is safe to resend.

id.description: Unique identifier for this article record, used for de-duplication on the server side when the url alone is not stable.

outlet.description: Publication or site that ran the article (e.g. 'BleepingComputer', 'The Record').

published_at.description: UTC timestamp at which the article was published.

updated_at.description: UTC timestamp of the most recent revision of the article, if the outlet exposes one.

language.description: BCP 47 language tag of the article text (e.g. 'en', 'de').

summary.description: Short abstract of the article, either from the outlet or generated by the aggregator.

content.description: Full extracted article text, when licensing and the aggregator allow carrying it.

related_actors.description: Ids of the actors and groups in this payload the article covers.

tags.description: Free-form tags applied by the aggregator (e.g. 'ransomware', 'takedown', 'indictment').

name.description: Display name or title of the reference.

provenance.title: Provenance
provenance.description: Where and when the aggregator retrieved this specific record.

retrieved_at.description: UTC timestamp at which the aggregator fetched this record from upstream.

upstream_url.description: Direct URL to the upstream record this was derived from.

raw_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the raw upstream record, for change detection.
